just looked at Gunfire stage again and realized the boss did damage based on your current hp

Thais being visible to everyone in the twins story is due to her forcing her way back into everyones memories at the end of chapter 7 right? Otherwise I'm not sure why shes suddenly visible to everyone despite being in a even weaker state than she was in 7 due to the mother tree not existing in the world anymore

thais never forced her way back into memory, keeper just told everyone about her after chapter 7
thais being in the twin story is a bit confusing tbh, but i interpret it as her lingering spiritual remnants in remouache after mythag took her corpse
if you remember the prologue of ch7 even czort didnt know the identity of the corpse in the coffin until keeper reminded them

I was moreso thinking about how no one could see her, not even the Aramites, until keeper mentioned her and suddenly everyone could see her and remember again. Its tricky when it comes to her cause Yesod slows dissolution iirc, so shes always this weird state of having dissoluted to the world but not herself
But yeah her showing up there is certainly confusing, it probably is just remnants of her will due to her capacity as a proxy while we work on awakening her with an actual body again
keeper has shown to have the unique ability to remember those affected by dissolution regardless of severity

and with how dissolution works is that the person affected could still be moving around like usual (till they drop dead), but the world will stop remembering and later perceiving them over time as it progresses
castor is a good example of how it works
he remembers pollux, but pollux doesnt remember him
thais case was very late stage where her existence was already being erased, hence why no one could even sense her presence
juliette is implied to also have a degree of memory dissolution resistance, with how she could remember castor and sense thais' influence on the aramites

i know the keeper can always remind people. But Thais was a unique case wasnt she? It happened to the mother tree too, no? No one other than the aramites could even see the mother tree at first unless my own mind is playing tricks on me, its been a while since i did chapter 7...
Castor and Pollux is a good example of the memories though youre right, I am curious though, are there any other cases of someone being imperceivable while still alive and present other than Thais?
the mother tree in theory could be seen by everyone, but since the storm wall was blocking it for so long, it essentially became folklore
other than thais, its hard to say, but i think that is more in the realm of "by the time anyone would have reached thais' stage of dissolution they would be dead first"
I had to go double check, but at first only we and the aramites could see it

Its just that it was in the same situation as thais
Dissoluted to the world, but still alive due to Tifere-Yesod slowing dissolution.

Not quite sure how the aramites were able to see it and not her, but probably due to the fact it wasnt truly gone, and neither was thais, its just no one remembered thais until we brought it up in front of the tree and everyone could see her again
ah yeah here
All of this makes sense and then she shows up in the twins story and i cant figure it out lmao
I guess shes visible cause shes remembered now, and it really is just her remnants wandering to do her job before she gets awakened again
I'm new to the game,I'm curious to know is the story is any good or not 👀 is it good?
Just read ch1 and then decide for yourself after the epilogue
I have. It wasn't bad ngl
Gameplay is cool and i love the artstyle
id say the writing is decently solid across the entire story, though it may get a bit confusing around the ch7-ch9 part due to the writers rushing stuff

is there an event that explains how what helot catena did to william somehow transported him into astral reign world, since they mention in chapter 4 and 5 that the william in that world is the exact same one from the world in faded legacy where he died in ch7

because they also mention in ch3 of astral reign that his mentor found the door in-between worlds which is how he just vanished (don't remember him getting mentioned in main story of faded legacy though), but how can he have accessed it if he got presumably bludgeoned by helot's floating balls and died in the tent
hasnt been explained yet, and even william admits he's not sure either.
i trust the writers will explain it down the line in arc 2 though
i see
is there also a reason why we got shota william in ch2 or nah
I don't think they ever said why only william out of everybody there had a different appearance in clementine's space
clem's domain eroded his mental state which was physically reflected, like how she turned the other patients into bugs
He really did just get killed so suddenly he got isekai'd huh
